title: salix × ambigua var. prostrata
intro: Salix × ambigua var. prostrata is a weeping willow cultivar, derived from the hybrid of Salix alba and Salix fragilis, known for its low-growing, prostrate habit. The medicinal properties of Salix × ambigua var. prostrata lie in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. This plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herbal preparations of Salix × ambigua var. prostrata include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
intro_study: According to "Frontiers in pharmacology", salix × ambigua var. prostrata benefits from the genus Salix's wide range of pharmacological activities, including analgesic, anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to its biologically active compounds such as salicin.
